description_long: This is a short section of a wooden picket fence, featuring five vertical pickets with pointed tops and two horizontal rails connecting them. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain patterns and some greenish discoloration, suggesting exposure to the elements. The fence is supported by two thicker, square posts at each end, which are slightly taller than the rails but not capped with any decorative finials. The pickets are evenly spaced, allowing visibility and airflow through the gaps. The overall construction is simple and utilitarian, with no ornamental features or embellishments. This type of fence is commonly found in residential yards, parks, or gardens to demarcate boundaries or provide a modest barrier.
description: A weathered wooden picket fence section with five pointed pickets, two horizontal rails, and two sturdy end posts. The design is simple and functional, with visible wood grain and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing five pointed pickets, two horizontal rails, and two square posts; wood grain and weathering are visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the fence, with the pickets and rails aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the same five pickets and two rails, with wood texture visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and alignment of the pickets and rails.
